source_pr.title: statistics: support gc outdated historical stats
source_pr.body:

### What problem does this PR solve?

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #xxx

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

support gc outdated historical stats

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test


### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: No explicit flaky failure signature is documented; the PR adds support for GC-ing outdated historical stats and corresponding tests.
failure_signature: Feature/change PR (historical stats GC + new sysvar) without a concrete flaky CI failure signature.
root_cause_explanation: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: The patch introduces/extends historical stats GC behavior (including a new duration sysvar) and adds a new unit test that uses `time.Sleep` to let stats become "outdated" before calling `ClearOutdatedHistoryStats()`. The PR does not provide CI logs or evidence of an intermittent flaky failure being fixed, so we conservatively classify it as insufficient evidence rather than guessing a flakiness root cause.
fix_pattern: Feature/change for historical stats GC + new sysvar/test; no explicit flaky failure signature/evidence provided.

evidence: evidence: tk.MustQuery(fmt.Sprintf("select count(*) from mysql.stats_meta_history where table_id = '%d' order by create_time", (executor/historical_stats_test.go)

Changed files (4): executor/historical_stats_test.go, sessionctx/variable/sysvar.go, sessionctx/variable/tidb_vars.go, statistics/handle/gc.go

Picked test: type=unit path=executor/historical_stats_test.go name=historical_stats_test
